seo.title: Journal — ArdenNest | Moments from the Ardennes
seo.description: Updates, seasonal atmosphere and stories from ArdenNest. Discover what's happening at our holiday home in the Ardennes.

heading: Journal
subtitle: Moments, updates and seasonal atmosphere — occasionally, when it matters.

petanquebaan-aangelegd.title: New pétanque court installed
petanquebaan-aangelegd.excerpt: The garden just got a little bigger. Perfect for a game of boules after dinner.
petanquebaan-aangelegd.body:
We'd been thinking about it for a while, and this winter was the moment: a proper pétanque court in the garden. Level ground, fine gravel, lit for evening games. Perfect for a relaxed match after dinner — while the kids still run around and the wine slowly empties.

The court is sheltered next to the terrace, walking distance from the kitchen. Balls are ready in the utility room. No reservation needed, no cleanup afterwards.

lente-in-de-ardennen.title: Spring has arrived in Gedinne
lente-in-de-ardennen.excerpt: The first blossoms, morning mist over the valley, and a garden coming back to life.
lente-in-de-ardennen.body:
March in the Ardennes has something special. The morning mist hangs low over the valley, the first crocuses poke through, and the birds are back. The garden slowly turns green again — cautiously at first, then suddenly everywhere.

For those seeking silence: this is the season. The hiking trails are empty, the forests smell of damp earth, and in the evening you hear only the owls. A weekend in March or April might just be the Ardennes' best-kept secret.

kerstweekend-met-26.title: Christmas weekend with 26 people
kerstweekend-met-26.excerpt: Three generations around the table. Exactly what this house was built for.
kerstweekend-met-26.body:
Christmas 2025 was a full house — and exactly as it should be. 26 people, three generations, from toddlers to grandparents. The professional kitchen was running at full capacity, the 7m20 dining table was truly full for once, and the dishwashers proved their worth once again.

What always strikes us: the house works best when it's full. The spaces are designed to let large groups live together without being on top of each other. The kids had their own world, the cooks their own domain, and anyone wanting quiet found it in one of the sitting corners.

This is what we do it for. Not for the perfect Christmas photo, but for the moment when everyone sits at the table and it goes quiet for a second.
